text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Renata Okonkwo is a 56-year-old female with a history of Stage IV malignant melanoma (BRAF V600E-mutant, M1c at presentation) arising from a dysplastic nevus of the mid-back. She has been receiving Opdivo maintenance (480 mg IV every 4 weeks) following prior treatment with dabra/Mekinist (discontinued at 8 months due to progression) and Yervoy/Opdivo (3 cycles, complicated by Grade 3 immune-related colitis requiring infliximab; Yervoy permanently discontinued). She is now approximately 21 months total into her immunotherapy course, 17 months into nivo monotherapy maintenance. Imaging performed for interval restaging assessment.

TECHNIQUE:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis: Multidetector CT acquired from the thoracic inlet through the symphysis pubis following intravenous administration of 90 mL iohexol (Omnipaque 350) with portal venous phase acquisition through the abdomen and pelvis. Multiplanar reconstructions generated. Slice thickness 5 mm.

MRI brain: Multiplanar, multisequence imaging of the brain performed at 3.0 Tesla including axial DWI, FLAIR, T2, T1 pre-contrast, and post-gadolinium T1-weighted sequences (axial, sagittal, and coronal planes) following administration of 7 mL gadobutrol (Gadavist) IV. No acute contraindications to gadolinium identified.

COMPARISON: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis from approximately 3 months prior (15 months total on immunotherapy), and prior MRI brain from approximately 6 months prior.

---

CT CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear. No parenchymal nodules, consolidation, ground-glass opacification, or pleural effusions. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. No evidence of immune-related pneumonitis. The tracheobronchial tree is unremarkable.

Pleura and Chest Wall: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax. No pericardial effusion. Soft tissues of the chest wall are unremarkable. No subcutaneous nodularity to suggest cutaneous metastatic disease, in keeping with prior resolution.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is midline. The heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. The thymus is not enlarged. The aorta and main pulmonary artery are normal in caliber.

Neck/Thoracic Inlet: A residual right level II cervical lymph node is noted, measuring 0.3 cm in short axis (previously 0.5 cm on the prior CT). This is below size threshold for pathological lymphadenopathy and has continued to decrease. No other cervical adenopathy identified.

Axillae: The right axillary region contains a small lymph node measuring 0.5 cm in short axis (previously 0.6 cm on the prior study and 1.6 cm at the time of immunotherapy initiation). This is not pathologically enlarged and demonstrates a normal fatty hilum on this study. The left axilla is unremarkable. No new axillary adenopathy bilaterally.

Skeletal Structures (Thorax): Osseous structures of the thorax are intact. No lytic or sclerotic lesions suspicious for osseous metastatic disease. Mild degenerative changes at the lower thoracic spine, age-appropriate.

---

CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and contour. Hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal mass lesion of definite concern. A single residual hypodense focus is identified in segment 6 of the right hepatic lobe, measuring approximately 0.3 cm (previously 0.4 cm on the prior study, and up to 1.4 cm at the time of disease progression). This lesion is at the lower limit of confident characterization on CT. It remains stable to minimally decreased in size; no interval growth. A second focus previously identified in the right lobe is not visualized on this study. The biliary system is not dilated. The portal vein is patent. No ascites.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation at 11 cm. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Normal in size, contour, and parenchymal attenuation. The pancreatic duct is not dilated. No peripancreatic lymphadenopathy.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands are normal in morphology. No adrenal nodules or thickening. No evidence of adrenal metastatic disease.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys are normal in size and enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses. No ureterolithiasis. Given this patient's history of immune-related adverse events on nivo (prior colitis, prior hypothyroidism), the renal cortices are assessed for nephritis — no cortical thinning or abnormal enhancement pattern to suggest immune-related nephritis.

Bowel: The stomach, small bowel, and large bowel are unremarkable in caliber and wall thickness. Notably, no colonic wall thickening or mucosal hyperenhancement to suggest recurrent immune-related colitis (previously seen as mild diffuse colonic wall thickening at the post-colitis restaging study). Bowel appearance is now normal.

Retroperitoneum and Mesentery: No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy. The previously identified peritoneal nodule adjacent to the falciform ligament is not visualized on the current study (absent on prior study as well).

Pelvis: No pelvic lymphadenopathy. No ascites. The uterus and adnexa appear unremarkable. Bladder is decompressed but unremarkable. No pelvic soft tissue masses.

Subcutaneous Tissues (Abdomen/Pelvis): No subcutaneous nodularity. No cutaneous thickening suspicious for melanoma deposits in the soft tissues of the trunk, consistent with sustained resolution since the targeted therapy era.

---

MRI BRAIN:

Brain Parenchyma: No mass lesion, hemorrhage, or edema identified. No abnormal enhancement on post-gadolinium sequences. The brain parenchyma demonstrates normal signal intensity on T1, T2, and FLAIR sequences. No leptomeningeal enhancement. No sulcal FLAIR signal abnormality.

Cerebellum and Brainstem: Normal morphology and signal. No focal lesions. No abnormal enhancement in the posterior fossa.

Ventricles and Sulci: Ventricles are normal in size and configuration. Sulci are appropriate for patient age. No midline shift or mass effect.

Vascular Structures: The major intracranial arteries are patent on the MRA sequences (time-of-flight, not performed as a separate study but assessed on T2/FLAIR). No dural sinus thrombosis.

Pituitary Gland: Pituitary gland appears normal in size and morphology. No expansion of the sella or stalk thickening to suggest immune-related hypophysitis (of particular clinical relevance given her history of ipi exposure and pre-existing autoimmune history).

Extraaxial Spaces: No subdural collections. No epidural collections.

Skull Base and Calvarium: No lytic or infiltrative bony lesions. No thickening or irregularity of the skull vault.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. The right cervical lymph node continues to involute, now barely perceptible at 0.3 cm — essentially indistinguishable from a normal-sized node. The right axillary lymph node has decreased marginally to 0.5 cm with a normal fatty hilum, in keeping with sustained treatment effect.

2. Hepatic disease: The previously noted right lobe hepatic lesion (segment 6) has decreased to 0.3 cm and remains present but is at the threshold of confident detection on CT. A second previously identified right lobe focus is no longer visualized. The overall hepatic picture shows continued regression since the nadir of disease.

3. No peritoneal, mesenteric, retroperitoneal, or pleural disease. The falciform ligament peritoneal nodule, first resolved on prior imaging, remains absent.

4. No evidence of new or progressive disease anywhere in the thorax, abdomen, or pelvis.

5. No intracranial metastases on gadolinium-enhanced MRI brain. No radiographic evidence of immune-related hypophysitis or leptomeningeal disease.

6. No imaging findings to suggest active immune-related toxicity involving the lungs, bowel, or kidneys.

In summary, this study shows a near-complete treatment response with continued regression across all previously identified disease sites on Opdivo maintenance. The only residual finding is a sub-centimeter hepatic focus that has progressively decreased and may represent a small scar or treatment-related change. Intracranial surveillance remains negative.
